title: Tags, Signatures, Verification, and Release Identity · Independent Repository Trial
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer tags, signatures, verification, and release identity into a new release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.
Verify supported GPG, SSH, or X.509 signatures while separating cryptographic validity, key identity, trust policy, authorization, and time evidence.
Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G79 is a lab in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-release-source-artifact-link-4xv79l" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Release Source Artifact Link into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-release-source-artifact-link-4xv79l
# scenario-key: trace-4xv79l probe-l97vx4 boundary-v79l4x owner-4xv79lx fault-4xv79lz
# before-ref: main=a11ce4x candidate=b00b19l
# hypothesis: the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review
git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0
# expected: verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy
# observation: the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist
# changed-case: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce4x candidate=c0ffeel
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest
# transfer-limit: signature validity alone does not prove key ownership, authorization, build, or delivered bytes
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.why: Retrieving Gita Release Source Artifact Link through the concrete release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.

hints: Name the Gita Release Source Artifact Link precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G88 is a lab in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest. Competency probe: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A valid signed tag proves which artifact bytes users received and that the build was reproducible.
